Midterm

Author

Nishat

Setup

#install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)

library(glue)

#install.packages("rio")
library(rio)

library(fs)

#install.packages("patchwork")
library(patchwork)
download_file <- function(year) {
  link <- glue(
    "https://www.oregon.gov/ode/educator-resources/assessment/TestResults20{year}/pagr_schools_ela_raceethnicity_{year-1}{year}.xlsx"
  )
  
  rio::import(
    link,
    setclass = "tibble",
    na = c("-", "--", "*"))}

Part A.1 Downloading Clean Data

raw_data <- map_dfr(15:18, download_file)

clean_data <- raw_data |>
  filter(`Student Group` %in% c("White", "Hispanic/Latino")) |>
  select(
    academic_year = `Academic Year`,
    district = District,
    school = School,
    student_group = `Student Group`,
    grade_level = `Grade Level`,
    `Number Level 1`,
    `Number Level 2`,
    `Number Level 3`,
    `Number Level 4`
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Number Level"),
    names_to = "level",
    values_to = "n"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    level = str_remove(level, "Number Level "),
    n = as.integer(n)
  ) |>
  drop_na(n)

clean_data
# A tibble: 90,576 × 7
   academic_year district     school       student_group grade_level level     n
   <chr>         <chr>        <chr>        <chr>         <chr>       <chr> <int>
 1 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 3     1         2
 2 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 3     2         1
 3 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 3     3         2
 4 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 3     4         1
 5 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 6     1         2
 6 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 6     2         3
 7 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 6     3         2
 8 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 6     4         0
 9 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 7     1         2
10 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 Adrian Elem… Hispanic/Lat… Grade 7     2         2
# ℹ 90,566 more rows

Part A.2 Summarizing by district, year, group, and level

new_data <- clean_data |>
  group_by(academic_year, district, student_group, level) |>
  summarise(
    n = sum(n),
    n_schools = n_distinct(school),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    student_group = str_to_lower(student_group),
    student_group = str_replace_all(student_group, "/", "_")
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = student_group,
    values_from = n
  ) |>
  drop_na(hispanic_latino, white) |>
  arrange(academic_year, district, level)

new_data
# A tibble: 736 × 6
   academic_year district     level n_schools hispanic_latino white
   <chr>         <chr>        <chr>     <int>           <int> <int>
 1 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 1             1               6     8
 2 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 2             1               6    17
 3 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 3             1               6    23
 4 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61 4             1               1    22
 5 2014-2015     Astoria SD 1 1             3              33   114
 6 2014-2015     Astoria SD 1 2             3              49   144
 7 2014-2015     Astoria SD 1 3             3              43   270
 8 2014-2015     Astoria SD 1 4             3              15   150
 9 2014-2015     Bandon SD 54 1             3               4    57
10 2014-2015     Bandon SD 54 2             3               7    80
# ℹ 726 more rows

Part B.1: Estimating Achievement Gaps using group_by() |> nest() |> map()

#install.packages(c("RJafroc", "yesno"))
library(RJafroc)
library(yesno)

#install.packages("gapr-master", repos = NULL, type = "source")
library(gapr)
v_nested_data <- new_data |>
  group_by(academic_year, district) |>
  nest() |>
  mutate(
    estimate = map(
      data,
      ~estimate_v(.x, "white", "hispanic_latino")
    )
  ) |>
  unnest(estimate) |>
  select(academic_year, district, data, auc, v, v_se) |>
  mutate(method = "group_by_nest_map")

v_nested_data
# A tibble: 184 × 7
# Groups:   academic_year, district [184]
   academic_year district                data       auc      v     v_se method  
   <chr>         <chr>                   <list>   <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>   
 1 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61            <tibble> 0.282 -0.814 0.0728   group_b…
 2 2014-2015     Astoria SD 1            <tibble> 0.383 -0.419 0.00910  group_b…
 3 2014-2015     Bandon SD 54            <tibble> 0.574  0.265 0.0383   group_b…
 4 2014-2015     Beaverton SD 48J        <tibble> 0.284 -0.806 0.000363 group_b…
 5 2014-2015     Bethel SD 52            <tibble> 0.437 -0.226 0.00263  group_b…
 6 2014-2015     Brookings-Harbor SD 17C <tibble> 0.463 -0.132 0.00948  group_b…
 7 2014-2015     Canby SD 86             <tibble> 0.278 -0.832 0.00265  group_b…
 8 2014-2015     Central SD 13J          <tibble> 0.336 -0.598 0.00334  group_b…
 9 2014-2015     Creswell SD 40          <tibble> 0.429 -0.254 0.0160   group_b…
10 2014-2015     Culver SD 4             <tibble> 0.404 -0.345 0.0162   group_b…
# ℹ 174 more rows

Part B.1.1 Estimating achievement gaps using nest_by() |> summarise()

v_rowwise <- new_data |>
  nest_by(academic_year, district) |>
  summarise(
    estimate_v(data, "white", "hispanic_latino"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(method = "nest_by_summarise")

v_rowwise
# A tibble: 184 × 6
   academic_year district                  auc      v     v_se method           
   <chr>         <chr>                   <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>            
 1 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61            0.282 -0.814 0.0728   nest_by_summarise
 2 2014-2015     Astoria SD 1            0.383 -0.419 0.00910  nest_by_summarise
 3 2014-2015     Bandon SD 54            0.574  0.265 0.0383   nest_by_summarise
 4 2014-2015     Beaverton SD 48J        0.284 -0.806 0.000363 nest_by_summarise
 5 2014-2015     Bethel SD 52            0.437 -0.226 0.00263  nest_by_summarise
 6 2014-2015     Brookings-Harbor SD 17C 0.463 -0.132 0.00948  nest_by_summarise
 7 2014-2015     Canby SD 86             0.278 -0.832 0.00265  nest_by_summarise
 8 2014-2015     Central SD 13J          0.336 -0.598 0.00334  nest_by_summarise
 9 2014-2015     Creswell SD 40          0.429 -0.254 0.0160   nest_by_summarise
10 2014-2015     Culver SD 4             0.404 -0.345 0.0162   nest_by_summarise
# ℹ 174 more rows

Part B.2 Confirming Same Estimates

comparison <- v_nested_data |>
  select(academic_year, district, v_nested_data = v) |>
  left_join(
    v_rowwise |>
      select(academic_year, district, v_rowwise = v),
    by = c("academic_year", "district"))

comparison
# A tibble: 184 × 4
# Groups:   academic_year, district [184]
   academic_year district                v_nested_data v_rowwise
   <chr>         <chr>                           <dbl>     <dbl>
 1 2014-2015     Adrian SD 61                   -0.814    -0.814
 2 2014-2015     Astoria SD 1                   -0.419    -0.419
 3 2014-2015     Bandon SD 54                    0.265     0.265
 4 2014-2015     Beaverton SD 48J               -0.806    -0.806
 5 2014-2015     Bethel SD 52                   -0.226    -0.226
 6 2014-2015     Brookings-Harbor SD 17C        -0.132    -0.132
 7 2014-2015     Canby SD 86                    -0.832    -0.832
 8 2014-2015     Central SD 13J                 -0.598    -0.598
 9 2014-2015     Creswell SD 40                 -0.254    -0.254
10 2014-2015     Culver SD 4                    -0.345    -0.345
# ℹ 174 more rows
#ggplot
ggplot(comparison, aes(x = v_nested_data, y = v_rowwise)) +
  geom_point(
    color = "grey60",  
    alpha = 0.6,
    size = 2
  ) +
  geom_abline(
    intercept = 0,
    slope = 1,
    color = "#56B4E9",  
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Achievement Gap Estimates Are Equivalent Across Methods",
    subtitle = "Comparison of group_by() |> nest() |> map() and nest_by() |> summarise()",
    x = "Estimate from group_by() |> nest() |> map()",
    y = "Estimate from nest_by() |> summarise()"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Part B.3: Creating Plots for the first 100 districts

v_plot_data <- v_nested_data |>
  select(academic_year, district, v, v_se) |>
  left_join(
    new_data |>
      distinct(academic_year, district, n_schools),
    by = c("academic_year", "district"))

#first 100 districts
dists <- unique(v_plot_data$district)[1:100]

v_100 <- v_plot_data |>
  filter(district %in% dists)
#function for one district plot
plot_gap <- function(df, district_name) {
  schools_caption <- df |>
    summarise(n_schools = max(n_schools, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
    pull(n_schools)
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = academic_year, y = v, group = 1)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_errorbar(
      aes(ymin = v - 1.96 * v_se, ymax = v + 1.96 * v_se),
      width = 0.1
    ) +
    labs(
      title = glue("Achievement Difference: {district_name}"),
      subtitle = "Hispanic/Latino students compared with White students",
      caption = glue("Number of schools in district: {schools_caption}"),
      x = "Academic Year",
      y = "Achievement difference estimate"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}
#Method 1: group_by() \|\> nest() \|\> map()
plots_method_1 <- v_100 |>
  group_by(district) |>
  nest() |>
  mutate(
    plot = map2(data, district, plot_gap)
  )

plots_method_1
# A tibble: 71 × 3
# Groups:   district [71]
   district                data             plot      
   <chr>                   <list>           <list>    
 1 Adrian SD 61            <tibble [1 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 2 Astoria SD 1            <tibble [4 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 3 Bandon SD 54            <tibble [2 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 4 Beaverton SD 48J        <tibble [1 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 5 Bethel SD 52            <tibble [3 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 6 Brookings-Harbor SD 17C <tibble [4 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 7 Canby SD 86             <tibble [4 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 8 Central SD 13J          <tibble [4 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
 9 Creswell SD 40          <tibble [4 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
10 Culver SD 4             <tibble [4 × 4]> <ggplt2::>
# ℹ 61 more rows
#first plot
plots_method_1$plot[[1]]
`geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

Part B.3.1 Alternative method for same plots

#Method 2: nest_by() \|\> mutate()
plots_method_2 <- v_100 |>
  ungroup() |>
  nest_by(district) |>
  mutate(
    plot = list(plot_gap(data, district))
  ) |>
  ungroup()

plots_method_2
# A tibble: 71 × 3
   district                              data plot      
   <chr>                   <list<tibble[,4]>> <list>    
 1 Adrian SD 61                       [1 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 2 Amity SD 4J                        [2 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 3 Annex SD 29                        [1 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 4 Astoria SD 1                       [4 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 5 Bandon SD 54                       [2 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 6 Banks SD 13                        [1 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 7 Beaverton SD 48J                   [1 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 8 Bethel SD 52                       [3 × 4] <ggplt2::>
 9 Brookings-Harbor SD 17C            [4 × 4] <ggplt2::>
10 Butte Falls SD 91                  [1 × 4] <ggplt2::>
# ℹ 61 more rows
plots_method_2$plot[[1]]
`geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

Part B.4: Save plots into plots directory

dir_create("plots")

# Clean file names
plots_method_1 <- plots_method_1 |>
  mutate(
    file_name = glue(
      "plots/method_1_{str_replace_all(str_to_lower(district), '[^a-z0-9]+', '_')}.png"
    )
  )

plots_method_2 <- plots_method_2 |>
  mutate(
    file_name = glue(
      "plots/method_2_{str_replace_all(str_to_lower(district), '[^a-z0-9]+', '_')}.png"))

# Save method 1 plots
walk2(
  plots_method_1$file_name,
  plots_method_1$plot,
  ~ggsave(
    filename = .x,
    plot = .y,
    width = 7,
    height = 5))

# Save method 2 plots
walk2(
  plots_method_2$file_name,
  plots_method_2$plot,
  ~ggsave(
    filename = .x,
    plot = .y,
    width = 7,
    height = 5))

Extra Credit: Combine pairs of plots from both methods

combined_plots <- plots_method_1 |>
  select(district, plot_1 = plot) |>
  left_join(
    plots_method_2 |>
      select(district, plot_2 = plot),
    by = "district"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    combined_plot = map2(
      plot_1,
      plot_2,
      ~.x / .y +
        plot_annotation(
          title = "Comparison of Plots from Two Methods",
          subtitle = "Top: group_by() |> nest() |> map(); Bottom: nest_by() |> mutate()",
          theme = theme(
            plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 6)
          ))),
    file_name = glue(
      "plots/combined_{str_replace_all(str_to_lower(district), '[^a-z0-9]+', '_')}.png"))

combined_plots
# A tibble: 71 × 5
# Groups:   district [71]
   district                plot_1     plot_2     combined_plot file_name

  combined_plots$file_name,
  combined_plots$combined_plot,
  ~ggsave(
    filename = .x,
    plot = .y,
    width = 8,
    height = 9))
